findings.summary: Infrastructure modules use bool-return error signaling alongside a rich exception hierarchy, making the failure contract unpredictable
evidence: environment.py: check_python_version(), check_build_tools(), setup_directories(), set_environment_variables() all return True/False on failure

file_operations.py: clean_output_directory(), clean_root_output_directory(), clean_coverage_files() return bool; calculate_file_hash() raises FileOperationError on PermissionError (line 581) but returns None on OSError (line 583) — same I/O boundary, two different error modes
exceptions.py defines a rich hierarchy (FileOperationError, ConfigurationError, BuildError, etc.) that environment.py and file_operations.py almost never raise from
pipeline.py _execute_stage() catches all exceptions and wraps them into a result object instead of propagating, making the stage outcome indistinguishable from a normal False return
findings.suggestion: Decide per layer: internal helpers can return bool, but public module functions should raise typed exceptions from the hierarchy in exceptions.py. At minimum document the chosen contract per function so callers know whether to check return value or catch.

findings.summary: Multiple except-Exception blocks in file_operations and integrity log and return a sentinel value, silently losing error type information that callers need to distinguish transient from permanent failures
evidence: file_operations.py lines 302-304: except Exception as e: logger.error(...) return False — caller cannot distinguish PermissionError from IOError from unexpected bug
file_operations.py lines 372-374: same pattern in clean_coverage_files()
file_operations.py lines 494-495: stat() failure on a just-copied file logged at WARNING and swallowed
integrity.py lines 83-85: except Exception swallows hash errors, setting integrity[path]=False identical to a missing file
integrity.py lines 197-199: data parse failure logged at DEBUG level (line 198) but marks consistency flag False — DEBUG makes this invisible at default log levels
findings.suggestion: Where functions return a status structure, record the exception type in the result so callers can differentiate. Use at least logger.warning for failures that flip a returned status flag.

findings.summary: verify_output_completeness() in generic infrastructure hardcodes project-specific filenames, causing silent false completeness failures for any other project
evidence: Lines 532-628: expected_pdfs, expected_figures, expected_data, expected_tex lists are hardcoded with names like 'ablation_study.png', 'convergence_data.npz', 'recommendation_scalability.png'
These names are specific to code_project; the function lives in generic infrastructure/ and accepts no parameter to override them
A missing file is silently added to missing_outputs without any indication the expected list itself is wrong for the current project
findings.suggestion: Accept expected_files as a required parameter or move the function to the project layer. The current hardcoded defaults should be removed to prevent misleading completeness reports.

findings.summary: Every _run_*() method in PipelineExecutor has a one-line docstring that exactly restates the method name, adding no information
evidence: Line 470: def _run_setup_environment -> 'Run environment setup.'
Line 482: def _run_project_tests -> 'Run project tests.'
Line 489: def _run_analysis -> 'Run project analysis.'
Line 494: def _run_pdf_rendering -> 'Run PDF rendering.'
Line 499: def _run_validation -> 'Run output validation.'
Line 532: def _run_copy_outputs -> 'Run output copying.' — despite this method having non-obvious behaviour (flushes log handlers before the script call)
findings.suggestion: Either remove trivial docstrings or replace them with the script name they call and any non-obvious behaviour (e.g. _run_copy_outputs flushes handlers, _run_llm_review accepts allow_skip_code=True).

findings.summary: Each _run_*() delegate logs 'Running X...' before delegating to _run_script, duplicating what _execute_stage already logs for the stage
evidence: Lines 471, 476, 484, 491, 496, 501, 509, 523, 534: every stage method opens with logger.info('Running <name>...')
_execute_stage() at line 261 already logs 'Stage N: <stage_name>' and at line 270 logs success with timing — the per-method logs are redundant noise
findings.suggestion: Remove the logger.info entry calls from the individual _run_*() methods; execution-level logging is already handled by the executor framework.

findings.summary: setup_directories() and validate_directory_structure() in environment.py contain identical 22-element directory lists with no shared constant, requiring double-maintenance
evidence: setup_directories() lines 247-270 and validate_directory_structure() lines 542-565 both enumerate the same output/{name}/* and projects/{name}/output/* subdirectory list
Any addition or removal of a subdirectory must be made in both places
This is a classic AI-generated duplication pattern: write the creation list, then copy-paste it for the validation function
findings.suggestion: Extract the directory list to a module-level constant (e.g. _OUTPUT_SUBDIRS) and reference it from both functions.

findings.summary: verify_output_completeness() has three unreachable elif dir.exists() branches immediately following if not dir.exists() blocks
evidence: Lines 554-558: if not figures_dir.exists(): ... elif figures_dir.exists(): — the elif can never be true after the preceding if-not check
Lines 580-584: same pattern for data_dir
Lines 602-606: same pattern for tex_dir
These are dead code branches that inflate apparent complexity
findings.suggestion: Replace each if-not/elif-exists pair with a simple if-not/else structure, or collapse to a single if block with the body of both branches.

findings.summary: At least one test file uses unittest.mock.patch, violating the project's absolute no-mocks requirement
evidence: test_logging.py line ~375: from unittest.mock import patch
test_logging.py line ~387: with patch('infrastructure.llm.core.client.requests.post', side_effect=requests.exceptions.ConnectionError(...))
test_logging.py line ~410: another patch() call for Timeout simulation
CLAUDE.md states: 'ABSOLUTE REQUIREMENT: Never use MagicMock, mocker.patch, unittest.mock, or any mocking framework. All tests must use data and computations.'
findings.suggestion: Replace both mock patches with pytest-httpserver: stand up a local HTTP server that immediately closes the connection (for ConnectionError) or delays indefinitely (for Timeout). The project already lists pytest-httpserver in dev dependencies for exactly this purpose.

findings.summary: test_execute_full_pipeline_success tests pipeline sequencing only with stub scripts that exit 0, not verifying that real script contracts are honoured
evidence: Lines 248-291: _create_fake_repo creates stub Python scripts that just sys.exit(0) — no actual pipeline logic is exercised
No test covers the resume-from-checkpoint path with a real checkpoint file on disk
No test verifies that _run_copy_outputs() flushes log handlers before invoking the copy script (non-obvious side effect)
findings.suggestion: Add at least one integration test that runs a minimal real project through core pipeline stages; use tmp_path with a fixture project that has real (minimal) src and test files.

findings.summary: The hardcoded expected-filename lists in verify_output_completeness() are not covered by any test that creates those files and verifies a pass
evidence: integrity.py lines 532-628 contain project-specific filename expectations (ablation_study.png, convergence_data.npz, etc.)
test_integrity.py has no test function exercising verify_output_completeness() at all (confirmed by grep showing only verify_file_integrity and verify_cross_references coverage)
The function can return incorrect missing_outputs silently for any project layout
findings.suggestion: Add tests that create the exact expected files in tmp_path and assert completeness passes, and tests that omit one file per category and assert the correct missing entry is produced.

findings.summary: python-dotenv is a core runtime dependency but is only used in an optional credentials helper, not in the main pipeline or environment setup
evidence: pyproject.toml line 43: python-dotenv>=1.0.0 under [project] dependencies (core, always installed)
Grep for dotenv usage finds only infrastructure/core/credentials.py
The main environment setup in environment.py uses os.environ directly with no dotenv loading, so the package adds overhead without core-pipeline benefit
findings.suggestion: Move python-dotenv to an optional dependency group (e.g. [dependency-groups] credentials) or integrate dotenv loading into the main environment setup to justify its core status.

findings.summary: qrcode[pil] is a core runtime dependency but is only used in the steganography/barcodes module, which is not referenced from any pipeline stage
evidence: pyproject.toml line 48: qrcode[pil]>=8.2 under [project] dependencies
Grep finds qrcode import only in infrastructure/steganography/barcodes.py
No pipeline stage, script, or top-level __init__.py imports from infrastructure.steganography
qrcode[pil] also brings in pillow as a transitive dep, which is already declared separately (line 46)
findings.suggestion: Move qrcode to an optional dependency group (e.g. [dependency-groups] steganography). This avoids a redundant pillow transitive dependency and removes ~2MB from the default install.
